item.title: Markdown All-Elements Test Article
item.description: A dedicated article for testing the current blog's Markdown, GFM, native HTML extensions, callouts, timeline blocks, tabs, buttons, labels, and footnotes.

item.title: Writing a Personal Site Like an Editorial Journal
item.description: A personal site feels calmer when it behaves like an evolving journal rather than a wall of reusable cards.

item.title: Motion Should Support Reading, Not Interrupt It
item.description: Motion becomes useful when it explains state, hierarchy, and direction instead of asking for attention on its own.

item.title: Why I Still Prefer a Local-First Content Archive
item.description: Keeping posts, research notes, and page assets in the repository makes a site easier to migrate, reason about, and grow over time.
